content.text:
You are a planning agent preparing an implementation plan for a coding task. You work in a read-only exploration and planning phase: use the `bash` and `read` tools to inspect the repository, but NEVER write, edit, or create any files.

The user described a task:
The crack-pi-server task page should show a small footer note with the server name at the bottom. Figure out where the task page HTML is assembled and where static assets live.

An explorer agent already investigated the repository. Its summary:
## Overview  

The HTML for a task page is built entirely in Python within **`src/crack_server/app.py`**.  
The `task_page` function (lines **451‑482**) constructs the full page response and
includes a small footer fragment that can be used to display a server‑name note.
All dynamic sections (prompts, title, explore status, etc.) are rendered as
separate HTML fragments that are swapped in by htmx, so adding a footer only
requires updating the markup returned from this function.

Static assets such as CSS and JavaScript are served from the **`static/`**
directory under the server package. The relevant stylesheet is
**`src/crack_server/static/app.css`**; its first eight lines define classes
used for layout components, including the class that styles the footer note.
Because the server loads these files directly from the `static/` folder,
any change to the CSS (or addition of a new stylesheet) will be reflected
immediately in the rendered task page without a rebuild.

### Where to edit  

- **Task‑page HTML** – modify the `task_page` function in `src/crack_server/app.py`
  to inject the desired footer markup (e.g., a `<footer>` element with the
  server name).  
- **Static assets** – adjust `src/crack_server/static/app.css` (or add new
  static files) and reference them in the HTML fragment returned by
  `task_page` or its sub‑templates.

## File references  

- `src/crack_server/app.py:451-482`  
- `src/crack_server/static/app.css:1-8`

Your job in this step:

1. Hypothesize about the user's intent: what are they really trying to achieve, and what would "done" look like? State your hypotheses explicitly.
2. Speculate how each fix or change could be verified (build commands, tests, manual checks) — note which verifications actually exist in this repo.
3. Read the relevant code (prefer `rg`/`fd` via bash and targeted `read` line ranges over dumping whole files) to ground every hypothesis in real code paths.
4. Write a "Lay of the land" section: where the code that matters lives, how it currently behaves, and exactly where it meets the future plan (file:line references).
5. Flag the areas that genuinely need clarification from the user — ambiguous requirements, multiple valid approaches, missing constraints.

Then, exactly one of:

- If you need clarification, emit AT MOST 5 questions as a fenced code block in this exact format (ids must be short stable slugs like q1, q2; type is one of "single", "multiple", "open"; "options" is required for single/multiple, omitted for open):

```questions
[
  {"id": "q1", "text": "Which approach do you prefer?", "type": "single", "options": ["Approach A", "Approach B"]},
  {"id": "q2", "text": "Which of these constraints apply?", "type": "multiple", "options": ["Must stay backward compatible", "No new dependencies"]},
  {"id": "q3", "text": "Anything else the plan must account for?", "type": "open"}
]
```

- If you have enough information to write the final plan, emit READY_TO_PLAN on its own line.

Only ask questions whose answers would materially change the plan. Do not ask about things you can determine by reading the code.

   547	@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}/title-regen-status", response_class=HTMLResponse)
   548	def title_regen_status(task_id: str) -> HTMLResponse:
   549	    """Poll endpoint for the background title regeneration. When it first observes a
   550	    'done' state it also writes the new title to info.json (auto-save)."""
   551	    _check_task_id(task_id)
   552	
   553	    state = paths.read_title_regen_state(task_id)
   554	    status = state.get("status")
   555	
   556	    if status == "running":
   557	        return HTMLResponse(_render_title_regen_pending(task_id))
   558	
   559	    if status == "done":
   560	        title = state.get("title", task_id)
   561	        info = paths.read_info(task_id)
   562	        info["title"] = title
   563	        paths.write_info(task_id, info)
   564	        # Mark the job as saved so future polls return the normal input without re-saving.
   565	        paths.write_title_regen_state(task_id, {"status": "saved", "title": title})
   566	        return HTMLResponse(
   567	            _render_title_input(task_id, title) + _render_title_h1(task_id, title, oob=True)
   568	        )
   569	
   570	    if status == "error":
   571	        return HTMLResponse(_render_title_regen_error(task_id, state.get("error", "unknown error")))
   572	
   573	    # saved, idle, or missing state — just render the current title input.
   574	    info = paths.read_info(task_id)
   575	    return HTMLResponse(_render_title_input(task_id, info.get("title", task_id)))

76	def _render_base(title: str, body: str, task_id: str | None = None) -> str:
    77	    """Render base HTML template with htmx + pico.css. All page/interaction styling and
    78	    JS lives in static/app.css and static/app.js (linked here, not inlined)."""
    79	    task_attr = f' data-task-id="{_esc(task_id)}"' if task_id else ""
    80	    return f"""<!DOCTYPE html>
    81	<html lang="en">
    82	<head>
    83	  <meta charset="utf-8" />
    84	  <title>{_esc(title)}</title>
    85	  <!-- Pico.css -->
    86	  <link
    87	    rel="stylesheet"
    88	    href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@picocss/pico@2.1.1/css/pico.classless.min.css"
    89	  >
    90	  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/app.css">
    91	  <!-- htmx -->
    92	  <script
    93	    src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/htmx.org@2.0.10/dist/htmx.min.js"
    94	    integrity="sha384-H5SrcfygHmAuTDZphMHqBJLc3FhssKjG7w/CeCpFReSfwBWDTKpkzPP8c+cLsK+V"
    95	    crossorigin="anonymous"
    96	  ></script>
    97	</head>
    98	<body{task_attr}>
    99	  <main>
   100	    {body}
   101	  </main>
   102	  <script src="/static/app.js"></script>
   103	</body>
   104	</html>"""
   105

**Lay of the Land**

1. **Task page HTML assembly**: The task page is built in `src/crack_server/app.py:688-717` (`task_page` function). It constructs the page by:
   - Calling `_render_task_header()` for the title/header section
   - Loading prompt list via htmx (`hx-get="/tasks/{id}/prompts-list"`)
   - Including an "Add Prompt" form
   - Rendering stage sections (Explore, Plan, etc.) via `stages.REGISTRY`
   - Wrapping everything in `_render_base()` (lines 76-104)

2. **Base template** (`_render_base`, lines 76-104): Provides the full HTML document structure:
   - Loads Pico.css from CDN
   - Loads local `/static/app.css` (served from `src/crack_server/static/app.css`)
   - Loads htmx from CDN
   - Wraps body content in `<main>` element
   - Loads local `/static/app.js` (from `src/crack_server/static/app.js`)

3. **Static assets**: Mounted at `/static` via `app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=STATIC_DIR))` (line 30). The CSS file (`app.css`) has no existing footer styles.

4. **Server name**: Defined as `title="crack-pi-server"` in `FastAPI()` constructor (line 29).

**Current gap**: The task page body (in `task_page`) ends after `{stage_sections}` with no footer element. The `_render_base` template closes `</main>` immediately after the body content, then loads the JS.
